summary:
To measure an individual’s arch perimeter effectively, one should use tools that allow for exact circular fitting; thus, option B, using brass wire, is most appropriate as it enables precise contouring along the curve of the foot. Options involving imaging or non-circular measures are less suited for this specific task. \(\Box\) ) provides the necessary flexibility and precision needed for such detailed anatomical assessments without requiring complex equipment. Therefore,

To determine the correct method for assessing arch perimeter directly and accurately through tactile means, brass wire stands out because its malleability allows conforming closely to the shape being measured—making it ideal compared to other listed alternatives which do not offer similar adaptability or accuracy. Hence,\( \boxed{\text{Brass Wire}} \) remains the optimal choice.\(\Box\)
